goal: Smoke-test navigate_to on Nova Carter and confirm arrival pose without involving Nav2.
thoughts: Use the sim-side go-to-pose primitive — not Nav2, not a full planner. The primitive is straight-line-ish with the built-in diff-drive controller; it does NOT path-plan around columns. Be upfront: if Sarah's goal is behind an obstacle she needs her own planner publishing /cmd_vel (which is fully supported — just don't call navigate_to and external /cmd_vel at the same time). Verify arrival by reading world transform.
code:
# 1. Confirm scene + robot present
summary = scene_summary()

# 2. Dispatch a reachable goal pose
result = navigate_to(
    robot_prim="/World/Carter_01",
    target_xy=[<GOAL_X>, <GOAL_Y>],
    target_yaw=<GOAL_YAW_RAD>,
    max_linear_speed=<MAX_LIN>,
    max_angular_speed=<MAX_ANG>,
    timeout_s=<TIMEOUT_S>,
)

# 3. Verify arrival (not just trust the return code)
pose = get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Carter_01/chassis_link")
# Log delta so Sarah can eyeball it
run_usd_script(code="print('delta_xy', <GOAL_X>-pose.translation[0], <GOAL_Y>-pose.translation[1])")

# If Sarah wants her own planner: do NOT call navigate_to, just let the
# ROS2 /cmd_vel subscriber drive the diff-drive controller directly.
failure_modes: assistant claims navigate_to does full planning around obstacles
assistant forgets to verify arrival pose
assistant uses navigate_to and external /cmd_vel simultaneously (conflict)
